explanation:
Personal productivity combines psychology, neuroscience, and time management. Methods like GTD (Allen), Deep Work (Newport), Pomodoro, and OKRs are globally used because of their proven effectiveness.

## How It's Calculated

Your reading speed in words per minute (WPM).

This calculator applies the formula with the values you enter, checking typical ranges and alerting you if values seem unreasonable.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult with a qualified professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: What is a good reading speed?
faq.a: Average adult reading speed is 200-250 WPM. Good speed is 300-400 WPM. Excellent speed is 500+ WPM.

faq.q: How do I calculate my reading speed?
faq.a: Divide the total number of words by the time in minutes: WPM = Words ÷ Minutes.
Average adults read 200-250 WPM. College students typically read 250-300 WPM.
Reading speeds of 300-400 WPM are considered fast. 500+ WPM is very fast reading.
Best comprehension occurs at 250-350 WPM. Reading faster than 400 WPM may reduce understanding.

faq.q: How can I improve my reading speed?
faq.a: Eliminate subvocalization, use chunking (reading groups of words), practice regularly, and reduce regression (backtracking).

faq.q: What's a realistic reading speed improvement?
faq.a: With consistent training, expect to increase your reading speed by 20-30%.
Yes, all our calculators are completely free with no registration required.
